theory.intro: When a problem mixes grams, kilograms and tonnes, first convert everything to one unit, then add, subtract or compare.
facts: Choose **one** common unit before calculating — usually the smallest in the problem.
Convert the answer back to a sensible unit at the end if needed.
steps: Pick a common unit for all the masses.
Convert each mass to that unit.
Add, subtract or compare, then state the answer clearly.

foundation.q: Add $2\text{ kg}$ and $500\text{ g}$. Give the answer in grams.
Add $1\text{ kg}$ and $750\text{ g}$. Give the answer in grams.
Subtract $300\text{ g}$ from $1\text{ kg}$. Give the answer in grams.
Which is heavier: $1\text{ kg}$ or $900\text{ g}$?
Add $3\text{ kg}$ and $250\text{ g}$. Give the answer in grams.
Subtract $400\text{ g}$ from $2\text{ kg}$. Give the answer in grams.
Two parcels are $500\text{ g}$ and $1\text{ kg}$. What is the total in kilograms?
Which is heavier: $2\text{ kg}$ or $1800\text{ g}$?
Add $250\text{ g}$ and $250\text{ g}$ and $500\text{ g}$. Give the answer in kilograms.
A box is $1500\text{ g}$ and a book is $0.5\text{ kg}$. Total mass in kilograms?

development.q: A bag of apples is $1.5\text{ kg}$ and oranges are $750\text{ g}$. Total in grams?
A crate weighs $4\text{ kg}$; you remove $1250\text{ g}$. What remains, in kilograms?
A pallet holds $0.5\text{ t}$ and another $350\text{ kg}$. Total in kilograms?
Three bags are $800\text{ g}$, $1.2\text{ kg}$ and $500\text{ g}$. Total in grams?
A load is $1.8\text{ t}$; $600\text{ kg}$ is unloaded. What is left, in tonnes?
Order from lightest: $1.1\text{ kg}$, $980\text{ g}$, $1050\text{ g}$.
A parcel is $2\text{ kg } 300\text{ g}$; postage adds $400\text{ g}$ packaging. Total in grams?
From $5\text{ kg}$ of rice you use $1750\text{ g}$. How much is left, in grams?
Two trucks carry $1.25\text{ t}$ and $900\text{ kg}$. Combined load in kilograms?
A shelf holds $3.5\text{ kg}$. You add $250\text{ g}$ four times. New total in kilograms?

mastery.q: A box of $750\text{ g}$ tins must not exceed $5\text{ kg}$ total. How many tins fit?
A lift limit is $1\text{ t}$. It holds $650\text{ kg}$ of boxes and a $250\,000\text{ g}$ cart. Is it overloaded?
A recipe needs $1.2\text{ kg}$ flour. You have $800\text{ g}$. How many more grams are needed?
Four parcels each $1\text{ kg } 250\text{ g}$ are posted. Total mass in kilograms?
A bin holds $0.75\text{ t}$. After tipping out $300\text{ kg}$, what is left in tonnes?
Sam buys $500\text{ g}$ cheese, $1.5\text{ kg}$ meat and $250\text{ g}$ butter. Total in kilograms?
A van limit is $1.2\text{ t}$. It carries $4$ crates of $200\text{ kg}$. How much more mass can it take?
A bag of dog food is $2.5\text{ kg}$. A dog eats $300\text{ g}$ a day. After a week, how much is left, in grams?
Compare two loads: Load A is $1\text{ t } 50\text{ kg}$, Load B is $1050\,000\text{ g}$. Which is heavier?
A shipment is $3.6\text{ t}$ split equally into $8$ crates. Mass of one crate in kilograms?
